questions.prompt: Complete the introduction: "Hello! I ___ Salma, and this is my friend Rania."
questions.explanation: After the subject "I", the verb "to be" is always "am": I am Salma. "Are" goes with you/we/they, "is" goes with he/she/it, and "be" is the base form, which cannot follow a subject directly here.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word: "___ are my grandparents."
questions.explanation: "Grandparents" means two people, so we use the plural demonstrative "these": These are my grandparents. "This", "it" and "he" are all singular, so they cannot introduce two people.

questions.prompt: Complete: "My little sister ___ got a red schoolbag."
questions.explanation: "My little sister" = she, and with he/she/it we use "has got": she has got a red schoolbag. "Have got" is for I/you/we/they, and "am/is got" mixes the verb "to be" with "got".

questions.prompt: Complete: "Every morning, Omar ___ up at seven o'clock."
questions.explanation: "Every morning" describes a daily routine, so we use the simple present. Omar = he, so the verb takes -s: he gets up. "Get" forgets the -s, and "getting" or "is get" are not correct simple present forms.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We have our English lesson ___ Monday."
questions.explanation: With days of the week we use "on": on Monday. "At" is for clock times (at eight o'clock) and "in" is for parts of the day (in the morning).
